arguments.task: Open the iPayX documentation website and extract evidence about: 1) what iPayX is, 2) whether there are public API/developer docs, 3) authentication methods documented for API access, 4) pricing or access gating for API use, 5) API surface type such as REST or GraphQL, 6) any rate limit notes, and 7) whether there is any mention of MCP, Model Context Protocol, GitHub repository, or MCP server. Prefer first-party pages linked from the site such as docs, pricing, auth, changelog, support, or GitHub links. Return direct URLs visited and concise findings with quotations where possible. Do not use third-party blogs unless the site links to them.

input.thought: Inspect the official iPayX docs and site for API, auth, access, and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: AI-powered FX audit and payment tooling with developer APIs and MCP access for cross-border transaction checks.
raw_result.auth_notes: The Developers page shows Bearer token authentication and references a production API key sent in the Authorization header; evidence suggests token-style bearer auth, but the exact credential issuance flow is not fully verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public Pricing page lists a free Developer tier and public Developers page is accessible without sales contact, indicating self-serve entry access.

api_surface.breadth: Developers page indicates public REST endpoints, cURL examples, an embeddable widget, MCP server access, and public/production tools for transaction audit and related FX/payment checks, but full endpoint catalog breadth was not exhaustively verified from the gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Site navigation and Developers page indicate MCP support, including an MCP server URL at mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp.

raw_result.blocker: Public docs and a free tier appear available, but the exact self-serve API-key issuance/onboarding flow and full endpoint/rate-limit details were not fully verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Evidence gathered indicates tier-based monthly audit limits on pricing and a specific public-tool limit noted as '10/IP/7d'; a complete rate-limit policy was not verified.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official site exposes dedicated Developers, Pricing, and MCP navigation; Developers page mentions Bearer token auth, production API key usage in Authorization header, REST examples, embeddable widget support, and an MCP server endpoint.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The original hint docs URL returned a 404, and conclusions rely on the main site Developers/Pricing pages plus browser-extracted text rather than directly verified complete docs pages. Exact auth taxonomy, free-tier API capabilities, and full rate-limit details remain somewhat ambiguous.
